verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP5B) is canonically a peripheral protein of the mitochondrial inner membrane, matrix-facing, forming the catalytic F1 head of the mitochondrial ATP synthase complex. However, extensive literature documents its ectopic display on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane (ecto-ATP synthase / eATPsyn) across tumor cells (NSCLC, breast, prostate, colon, leukemia), vascular endothelial cells, and neuronal cells. The trafficking mechanism is established: the fully assembled complex is transported from mitochondria to the plasma membrane via microtubule-dependent DRP1/KIF5B machinery with mitochondrial membrane-PM fusion anchoring it there. Cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs include monoclonal antibodies (McAb4E7 in NSCLC), RNA aptamers (Apt63 targeting breast/prostate cancer), and angiostatin (an endogenous antagonist). This outer-leaflet pool is not constitutively present in all cells at baseline, but is robustly documented in tumor/activated endothelial/neuronal contexts, qualifying as cell_state_induced.
